findings.message: Duplicate data-access layer: the route imports `add_item`/`fetch_all_items` from a new `..db.item_service` module that persists the same `Item` model already served by `..db.repository`. The repository already exposes a `create_item(session, name)` (and `list_items`), so `item_service` is a redundant parallel persistence abstraction over the same entity. This fragments the data layer — two modules now own Item reads/writes, with divergent commit semantics (repository.create_item commits internally; item_service.add_item only flushes, forcing the route to commit/refresh itself).
findings.suggestion: Drop the `item_service` import and call the existing `create_item` from `..db.repository` (which already encapsulates session.add/commit/refresh). Remove `src/demo/db/item_service.py` so there is a single canonical repository for the Item entity, or consolidate any genuinely new helpers into `repository.py`.

findings.message: The new create endpoint routes persistence through `item_service.add_item` instead of the existing `repository.create_item`, then re-implements commit/refresh inline. This duplicates the create path that already lives in the repository and couples the route to a second data-access module, defeating the repository boundary the rest of the file follows (list_items).
findings.suggestion: Replace `item = add_item(session, payload.name); session.commit(); session.refresh(item)` with `item = create_item(session, payload.name)` from `..db.repository`, keeping all Item persistence behind a single layer.

findings.message: `fetch_all_items` is imported from `..db.item_service` but never used in this module.
findings.suggestion: Remove the unused `fetch_all_items` import (and prefer consolidating on the repository module per the layering finding above).
